headline: Panama Unlocks Copper Supply Critical to Data Center Infrastructure Build-Out
summary: Panama will authorize First Quantum to extract ore from the shuttered Cobre Panama mine, one of the world's largest copper deposits idled since late 2023. The move eases a meaningful constraint on global copper supply at a moment when AI-driven data center expansion is pushing demand to record levels. Resumption of exports could modestly soften copper prices and reduce input cost pressure on hyperscaler infrastructure procurement.
oci_implication: Copper is foundational to OCI's capital expansion — from power cabling to server components — so any supply relief supports cost predictability as OCI accelerates region build-outs to compete with AWS and Azure. Monitor whether this translates into improved hardware lead times or favorable pricing in upcoming infrastructure procurement cycles.
